allowed_headline: A reduced black-hole projection is operational in the tested simulation and survey branches.
Specific accretion rate is used provisionally as a forcing or throughput coordinate.
S is an equilibrium-distance coordinate only where code, configuration, and equation use a concordant residual construction.
The Tier 1 composite is exploratory until redundancy and noise-matched effective dimensionality are audited.
In the frozen TNG Tier 1 branch, residence-based ripeness has a negative dispersion slope under the declared estimators, subject to provenance, effective-unit, and construction-null caps.
The ordinary-variance median-potential trend is tail dominated and fails robust re-estimation.
The GAMA D4000-offset branch is a directional, three-bin, resolution-limited observational analogue.
The GAMA sigma-star construction is numerically quarantined pending clean reconstruction and target-side independence checks.
MaNGA demonstrates survey-scale portability but refuses a universal compression law.
The score is an exploratory contextual ranking whose component and rank stability can be audited.
Extreme settlement outliers are candidates for independent merger and host-disturbance validation.
Memory remains unactivated until a frozen generator ladder survives admitted finite augmentations and time-order nulls.
Recoverability remains unactivated until an information-time-valid R predicts an independent later recovery event.
The current evidence supports at most a reduced Tier 1 projection while R and M remain future tests.
